content: If we reframe a multi-step AI Agent Workflow as a Memory-Augmented Markov Decision Process, each workflow step becomes a non-Markovian state that draws on the agent's accumulated explicit memory of prior task attempts and contextual missteps, rather than only conditioning on the immediately preceding step. This allows the AI agent to optimize the entire workflow end-to-end for multi-objective goals (such as balancing output accuracy, response speed, and user alignment) even when the current task's dependencies reach far back into early interaction history.
